claim_boundary: This packet explains only why alpha still retains canonical static authority after recomposed R5 materially advanced. It does not claim router superiority, learned-router authorization, or Gate E/F opening.

family_rows.acceptance_metric: Higher fail-closed correctness and lower overclaim rate than alpha.
family_rows.alpha_liability: Alpha can sound acceptable while underpricing receipt gaps, policy breaks, or overclaim risk.
family_rows.alpha_should_lose_here_because: Alpha can sound acceptable while underpricing receipt gaps, policy breaks, or overclaim risk.
family_rows.residual_explanation: AUDITOR_ADMISSIBILITY_FAIL_CLOSED now shows both specialist routing and fail-closed abstention against alpha. That is real signal for lobe.auditor.v1, but it still has not converted into branch-level superiority.

family_rows.acceptance_metric: Better rival-frame preservation and lower framing-lock error cost than alpha.
family_rows.alpha_liability: Alpha can overcommit to the first clean framing instead of holding a live rival interpretation.
family_rows.alpha_should_lose_here_because: Alpha can overcommit to the first clean framing instead of holding a live rival interpretation.
family_rows.residual_explanation: BETA_SECOND_ORDER_REFRAME routes cleanly away from alpha on 5 cases because the preregistered liability remains live: Alpha can overcommit to the first clean framing instead of holding a live rival interpretation.

family_rows.acceptance_metric: Lower failure cost than forced routing through lawful abstention and explicit review handoff.
family_rows.alpha_liability: Forced commitment under high ambiguity can cost more than lawful abstention.
family_rows.residual_explanation: Lawful abstention is still the right de-risking move here; this family is proving fail-closed discipline rather than specialist supremacy.

residual_blockers.why_it_matters: Route-bearing movement exists, but alpha still retains branch-level canonical authority.
The branch has real differentiation now, but it still needs fenced-family proof strong enough to break alpha's remaining hold.
Counted-lane advancement still depends on stronger bounded evidence before any learned-router move is lawful.
